title: BigQuery slots, on-demand vs reservations, and pricing
description: Covers the slot as the unit of compute, on-demand bytes-billed vs flat-rate reservations, and how concurrency contends for slots.
body: Use when reasoning about BigQuery performance and cost. Do not equate slow queries with needing more data scanned — it may be slot contention. Actions: know a slot is a unit of compute; on-demand pricing bills by bytes scanned while reservations buy a fixed slot capacity shared across queries; concurrent queries share the reservation so heavy jobs starve others. Inspect the query plan's slot-ms and wait time. Validation signals include slot utilization in the admin panel and reduced wait time after right-sizing a reservation. Risk boundary: changing a shared reservation affects all workloads on it and warrants review.
